machinery_genes.quote: In-gel digestion followed by tandem mass spectrometry identified the protein as Hcp1 (PA0085).
machinery_genes.note: Founding H1-T6SS paper; Hcp1 is the diagnostic secreted tube protein of the HSI-I (H1-T6SS) apparatus.

machinery_genes.quote: The latter lacks the H1-T6SS ATPase, ClpV1, and therefore remains in the “off-state”.
machinery_genes.note: ClpV1 also named in Mougous 2006 (10.1126/science.1128393): “To distinguish the functions of ClpB and ClpV1 in P. aeruginosa, we determined the thermotolerance of ΔclpB and ΔclpV1.”

machinery_genes.quote: Each of the three P. aeruginosa T6SSs encodes a VipA and a VipB homologue, namely HsiB1 and HsiC1 (H1-T6SS).
machinery_genes.note: Lossi et al. 2013 (HsiB1C1 sheath structure paper, PMC3597794). HsiB1 = TssB = VipA homologue of the H1-T6SS.

machinery_genes.quote: HsiB1 and HsiC1 of the Pseudomonas aeruginosa H1-T6SS assemble into tubules resulting from stacking of cogwheel-like structures.
machinery_genes.note: HsiC1 = TssC = VipB homologue; forms the bacteriophage tail sheath-like structure with HsiB1.

machinery_genes.quote: In P. aeruginosa, three T6SSs are known. The expression of H1-T6SS genes is controlled by the RetS sensor. Here, 10 vgrG genes were identified in the PAO1 genome, among which three are co-regulated with H1-T6SS, namely vgrG1a/b/c.
machinery_genes.note: Hachani et al. 2011. VgrG1a (and VgrG1c) essential for Hcp1 secretion; trimeric tail-spike-like. VgrG1a/c are ClpV1-dependent H1-T6SS spike proteins.

machinery_genes.quote: We demonstrate that Hcp1 secretion requires either VgrG1a or VgrG1c, which may act independently to puncture the bacterial envelope and give Hcp1 access to the surface.
machinery_genes.note: VgrG1c co-regulated with and secreted by the H1-T6SS in a ClpV1-dependent manner; functionally redundant with VgrG1a for Hcp1 secretion.

machinery_genes.quote: Whereas VgrG1a and VgrG1c were secreted in a ClpV1-dependent manner, secretion of VgrG1b was ClpV1-independent.
machinery_genes.note: VgrG1b is co-regulated with H1-T6SS (vgrG1a/b/c) but secreted independently of ClpV1 and not required for Hcp1 secretion; listed as a VgrG-family spike protein of the locus with the noted caveat.

machinery_genes.quote: Two VgrG-family proteins, the products of open reading frames PA0091 and PA2685, were the most abundant C2 proteins in R1 and R2... The PA0091 locus is located within HSI-I, while the PA2685 locus is found at an unlinked site that lacks other apparent T6S elements... these genes will henceforth be referred to as [vgrG1 and vgrG4].
machinery_genes.note: PA0091 (VgrG1) is the VgrG encoded inside the HSI-I locus and exported by the H1-T6SS. (Hood et al. 2010 nomenclature; PA0091 corresponds to vgrG1, distinct from the unlinked vgrG1a/b/c naming of Hachani 2011 — see notes field.)

machinery_genes.role: trimeric spike exported by H1-T6SS (PA2685; unlinked locus)
machinery_genes.quote: These data show that the H1-T6SS exports at least two VgrG-family proteins.
machinery_genes.note: PA2685/VgrG4 is exported by the H1-T6SS though encoded outside the HSI-I locus; secretion is PppA-repressed and ClpV1-dependent (Hood 2010). Whitney 2014: “PA2685, which encodes VgrG4, a protein our group previously showed is exported by the H1-T6SS.”

machinery_genes.quote: Finally, the starfish-shaped dodecameric protein, TssA, limits contractile sheath polymerization.
machinery_genes.note: Liebl et al. 2019, H1-T6SS assembly study. Enumeration is of the conserved core within an explicit H1-T6SS context.

machinery_genes.quote: ...the baseplate proteins TssE, TssF, TssG, and TssK...
machinery_genes.note: Liebl et al. 2019, H1-T6SS. Corresponds to HSI-I locus baseplate genes.

machinery_genes.quote: ...the baseplate proteins TssE, TssF, TssG, and TssK...
machinery_genes.note: Liebl et al. 2019, H1-T6SS.

machinery_genes.quote: ...the baseplate proteins TssE, TssF, TssG, and TssK...
machinery_genes.note: Liebl et al. 2019, H1-T6SS.

machinery_genes.quote: ...the baseplate proteins TssE, TssF, TssG, and TssK...
machinery_genes.note: Liebl et al. 2019 study is specifically of the H1-T6SS TssK baseplate component; paper refers to it as TssK (locus tag in HSI-I).

machinery_genes.quote: Here, we present the X-ray crystal structure of TssJ1 from the H1-T6SS of P. aeruginosa.
machinery_genes.note: Robb et al. 2013, structure of TssJ1 (PMC3668576). Outer-membrane-associated lipoprotein essential for H1-T6SS function. Also named TssJ1 in Casabona et al. 2012 (10.1111/j.1462-2920.2012.02816.x).

machinery_genes.quote: ...the membrane associated complex TssJ-TssL-TssM...
machinery_genes.note: Liebl et al. 2019, H1-T6SS. TssJ-TssL-TssM trans-envelope membrane complex of the HSI-I apparatus.

machinery_genes.quote: ...the membrane associated complex TssJ-TssL-TssM...
machinery_genes.note: TssM = IcmF1 in original Mougous 2006 nomenclature: “we tested the dependence of Hcp1 secretion by ΔretS on HSI-I components by generating a deletion of icmF1” (10.1126/science.1128393, PMID 16763151).

machinery_genes.role: forkhead-associated scaffolding protein; phosphorylation triggers H1-T6SS assembly/Hcp1 secretion
machinery_genes.quote: Colocalization experiments with the T6S AAA+ family protein, ClpV1, indicate that Fha1 is a core scaffolding protein of the H-T6SS.
machinery_genes.note: Mougous et al. 2007. Fha1 phosphorylation by PpkA is required for apparatus assembly and Hcp1 export.

machinery_genes.role: transmembrane threonine kinase; phosphorylates Fha1 to activate H1-T6SS
machinery_genes.quote: We show that a serine-threonine kinase, PpkA, is required for assembly of the H-T6SS and for secretion of Hcp1.
machinery_genes.note: Mougous et al. 2007. Posttranslational regulator of the H1-T6SS; structural role confirmed in Hsu et al. 2009 (10.1111/j.1365-2958.2009.06701.x).

machinery_genes.role: Ser/Thr phosphatase antagonizing PpkA; negative regulator of H1-T6SS
machinery_genes.quote: PpkA activity is antagonized by PppA, a Ser-Thr phosphatase.
machinery_genes.note: Mougous et al. 2007. PppA dephosphorylates Fha1, holding the H1-T6SS in the off-state.

notes: H1-T6SS = HSI-I locus (Hcp Secretion Island I), the best-characterized of PAO1's three T6SSs; disambiguated throughout from H2-T6SS (HSI-II) and H3-T6SS (HSI-III), which are NOT included. Core apparatus fully covered: tube (Hcp1/TssD), sheath (HsiB1/HsiC1 = TssB/TssC = VipA/VipB), spike (VgrG1a/c trimeric, plus HSI-I-encoded VgrG1/PA0091 and H1-exported VgrG4/PA2685), ATPase (ClpV1/TssH), baseplate (TssE/F/G/K), membrane complex (TssJ1 / TssL / TssM=IcmF1), cap (TssA), plus the PpkA/PppA/Fha1 threonine-phosphorylation regulatory module. VgrG nomenclature caveat: Hood 2010 names the HSI-I-internal VgrG as vgrG1 (PA0091) and the unlinked H1-exported one as vgrG4 (PA2685); Hachani 2011 independently names three H1-co-regulated VgrGs vgrG1a/b/c. These naming schemes overlap; gene NAMES recorded as the papers use them, no locus_tags invented beyond those the papers themselves cite. PAAR: in PAO1's H1-T6SS, PAAR-repeat motifs are carried as N-terminal domains fused to the Tse5 (PA0093) and Tse6 (PA2684) EFFECTORS rather than as a discrete standalone machinery gene (Whitney 2014, 10.1111/mmi.12571). Because the brief classifies the cargo-bearing PAAR-toxins as effectors (Tse5/Tse6 are in the effector list as PA0093/PA2684) and no separate orphan PAAR apparatus gene is named in the H1-T6SS literature, PAAR is NOT listed as its own machinery row to avoid double-counting an effector; documented here for completeness. Additional accessory regulators not listed as separate rows but documented in the cited papers: TagR (Hsu 2009), TagQ/TagS/TagT (Casabona 2012, 10.1111/j.1462-2920.2012.02816.x) of the phosphorylation-dependent posttranslational activation cascade. Source attribution: gene identifications drawn from PubMed-indexed peer-reviewed literature (DOIs above).
